description: Across breeds and living arrangements, a domestic dog is 'lohau'. Temperament and relation with people are the next facts, not part of the species name. 'lorahu' (wolf) is its close wild relative; breed and legal categories keep the labels that define them.
articulatory_notes: Lateral 'l' touches the alveolar ridge and releases into rounded 'o'. An open glottis lets unvoiced 'h' pass into stressed 'a', followed by a separate high-back rounded 'u' across the hiatus.
sound_symbolism: The falling 'a.u' hiatus may recall a drawn-out canine call and offers a simple mnemonic for the word.
usage_notes: A count can insert optional 'lipha'. 'keloe' (companion) before 'lohau' makes that relation explicit, while a working role belongs in its own phrase.

semantic_domains.community: a dog in a stated household, companion, or work relation
semantic_domains.nature: the domestic dog across breeds and living arrangements
